src: κρινέσθω ὑπὸ ἀκροβύστων,
lit: Let him be judged by the uncircumcised,
sm: Let him be judged by the uncircumcised,

syn.mc: Third-person imperative clause in a hostile wish.
syn.svo: Subject: implied Christ/the victim. Verb: κρινέσθω. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ ἀκροβύστων.
syn.rhet: The line continues the violent imperative sequence.

src: προσηλούσθω ὑπὸ τυραννικῆς δεξιᾶς,
lit: Let him be nailed by a tyrannical hand,
sm: Let him be nailed by a tyrannical hand,

syn.mc: Parallel third-person imperative clause.
syn.svo: Subject: implied Christ/the victim. Verb: προσηλούσθω. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τυραννικῆς δεξιᾶς.
syn.rhet: The sentence escalates from judgment to crucifixion imagery.

src: ὑπὸ δὲ ἐμοῦ μή.
lit: but not by me.
sm: but not by me.

syn.mc: Elliptical antithetic tail to the preceding imperatives.
syn.sub: No finite clause; the verb is understood from lines 581-582.
syn.svo: No overt verb. Prepositional phrase: ὑπὸ ἐμοῦ. Negation: μή.
syn.rhet: The contrast tightens the blame by excluding the speaker from the violence.

src: σὺ δὲ ταύτην, ὦ ᾿Ισραήλ, πρὸς τὸν θεὸν οὐκ ἐβροησας τὴν φωνήν"
ana: σὺ δὲ ταύτην, ὦ Ἰσραήλ, πρὸς τὸν θεὸν οὐκ ἐβόησας τὴν φωνήν
lit: But you, O Israel, did not cry out this cry to God
sm: But you, O Israel, did not cry this cry to God
emd: Normalized mixed OCR in ᾿Ισραήλ and corrected ἐβροησας to ἐβόησας; removed trailing OCR quotation mark.

syn.mc: Main interrogative accusation.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἐβόησας. Object: τὴν φωνήν, intensified by ταύτην. Prepositional phrase: πρὸς τὸν θεόν.
syn.rhet: The emphatic repetition of 'this cry' sharpens the indictment.

src: οὐδὲ ἀφωσίωσαι τῷ δεσπότῃ;
lit: Nor did you consecrate it to the master?
sm: Nor did you consecrate it to the Master?
emd: Corrected syntactically broken ἀφωσίωσαι to the expected 2sg aorist ἀφωσίωσας.

syn.mc: Second interrogative accusation.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ understood from line 586. Verb: ἀφωσίωσας. Dative recipient: τῷ δεσπότῃ.
syn.rhet: The rhetorical questions press the failure to respond rightly to signs.

src: οὐδὲ ἐδυσωπήθης τὰ ἐργὰ αὐτοῦ.
lit: Nor were you moved by his works.
sm: Nor were you moved by his works.
emd: Normalized the corrupted accent on ἐργὰ to ἔργα.

syn.mc: Third interrogative accusation.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ understood from line 586. Verb: ἐδυσωπήθης. Object/cause: τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: The list moves from voice to consecration to visible works.

src: οὐκ ἐδυσώπησῶ σε χεὶρ ξηρὰ ἀποκατασταθεῖσα τῷ σώματι;
ana: οὐκ ἐδυσώπησέ σε χεὶρ ξηρὰ ἀποκατασταθεῖσα τῷ σώματι;
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg f
lit: Did not a dry hand, restored to the body, persuade you?
sm: Did not a dry hand, restored to the body, move you?
emd: Corrected the impossible ἐδυσώπησῶ to ἐδυσώπησέ.

syn.mc: Rhetorical question with a causal subject phrase.
syn.svo: Verb: ἐδυσώπησέ. Object: σε. Subject: χεὶρ ξηρά. Participial modifier: ἀποκατασταθεῖσα τῷ σώματι.
syn.ptc: ἀποκατασταθεῖσα describes the healed hand that should have moved the addressee.
syn.rhet: The question heightens the failure to recognize healing.

src: οὐδὲ ὀφθαλμοὶ πηρῶν διὰ χειρὸς ἀνοιγόμενοι,
tok: pres mp ptcp nom pl m
lit: Nor eyes of the blind, being opened through a hand,
sm: Nor the eyes of the blind, opened through a hand,

syn.mc: Further rhetorical list item.
syn.svo: Subject: ὀφθαλμοί. Genitive modifier: πηρῶν. Participle: ἀνοιγόμενοι. Instrumental phrase: διὰ χειρός.
syn.ptc: ἀνοιγόμενοι is attributive and describes the healing sign.
syn.rhet: The examples accumulate as a catalogue of unheeded miracles.

src: οὐδὲ λελυμένα σώματα διὰ φωνῆς ἀναπηγνύμενα.
tok: pf mp ptcp nom pl n
pres mp ptcp nom pl n
lit: Nor bodies loosed, being made firm again through a voice.
sm: Nor bodies loosed, restored through a voice.

syn.mc: Parallel list item continuing the signs.
syn.svo: Subject: σώματα. Modifier: λελυμένα. Participle: ἀναπηγνύμενα. Instrumental phrase: διὰ φωνῆς.
syn.rhet: The cadence remains deliberately cumulative.

src: οὐδὲ τὸ καινότερόν σε ἐδυσώπησεν σημεῖον,
lit: Nor did the more recent sign persuade you,
sm: Nor did the more recent sign move you,

syn.mc: Climactic rhetorical question.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ καινότερον σημεῖον. Verb: ἐδυσώπησεν. Object: σε.
syn.rhet: The phrase sums up the signs before the Lazarus example.

src: νεκρὸς ἐϊς μνημείου ἐγειρόμενος ἤδη τεσσάρων ἡμερῶν.
ana: νεκρὸς ἐκ μνημείου ἐγειρόμενος ἤδη τεσσάρων ἡμερῶν.
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: a dead man being raised from a tomb already four days [dead].
sm: a dead man being raised from the tomb, already four days dead.
emd: Corrected syntactically impossible ἐϊς to ἐκ.

syn.mc: Participial fragment naming the Lazarus sign.
syn.svo: Subject: νεκρός. Participle: ἐγειρόμενος. Prepositional phrase: ἐκ μνημείου. Genitive of duration: τεσσάρων ἡμερῶν.
syn.rhet: The most recent sign is specified as the raising of Lazarus.

src: σὺ μὲν οὖν ταῦτα παραπεμψάμενος
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: You therefore, having passed these things by,
sm: You therefore, having passed over these things,

syn.mc: Participial lead-in to the next finite clause.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Participle: παραπεμψάμενος. Object: ταῦτα.
syn.ptc: παραπεμψάμενος is circumstantial and concessive in force.
syn.rhet: The addressee is accused of disregarding the signs.

src: ἔσπευσας ἐπὶ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου σφαγήν.
lit: you hastened toward the slaughter of the Lord.
sm: you hastened to the slaughter of the Lord.

syn.mc: Main finite clause completing the participial phrase of line 594.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἔσπευσας. Prepositional object: ἐπὶ τὴν ... σφαγήν.
syn.rhet: The accusation turns from neglect to active participation.

src: ἡτοίμασας αὐτῷ ἥλους ὀξεῖς καὶ μάρτυρας ψευδεῖς
lit: You prepared sharp nails for him and false witnesses
sm: You prepared sharp nails for him and false witnesses

syn.mc: Main accusation clause with coordinated objects.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἡτοίμασας. Indirect object: αὐτῷ. Objects: ἥλους ὀξεῖς and μάρτυρας ψευδεῖς.
syn.rhet: Violence and false testimony are paired.

src: καὶ βρόχους καὶ μάστιγας"
lit: and nooses and scourges
sm: and nooses and scourges

syn.mc: Continuation of the object list from line 596.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Coordinated accusative objects: βρόχους and μάστιγας.
syn.rhet: The inventory of suffering expands relentlessly.

src: καὶ ὄξος καὶ χολὴν
lit: and vinegar and gall
sm: and vinegar and gall

syn.mc: Further continuation of the accusative list.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Coordinated objects: ὄξος and χολήν.
syn.rhet: The list moves from torture implements to bitter sustenance.

src: καὶ μάχαιραν καὶ θλῖψιν ὡς ἐπὶ φόνιον λῃστήν.
lit: and a sword and affliction, as against a murderous robber.
sm: and a sword and affliction, as if against a murderous robber.

syn.mc: Final item in the hostile inventory.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Objects: μάχαιραν and θλῖψιν. Comparative phrase: ὡς ἐπὶ φόνιον λῃστήν.
syn.rhet: The culmination of hostility is cast in criminal imagery.

src: ἐπηνέγκω γὰρ αὐτοῦ καὶ μάστιγας τῷ σώματιδ
ana: ἐπήνεγκας γὰρ αὐτοῦ καὶ μάστιγας τῷ σώματι
lit: For you also brought scourges upon his body.
sm: For you also brought scourges upon his body.
emd: Corrected raw ἐπηνέγκω to the expected 2sg aorist ἐπήνεγκας and removed stray final δ from σώματιδ.

syn.mc: Main clause continuing the accusation list.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἐπήνεγκας. Object: μάστιγας. Dative phrase: τῷ σώματι; genitive αὐτοῦ marks the body as his.
syn.rhet: The text now specifies bodily torment.

src: καὶ ἄκανθαν τῇ κεφαλῇ αὐτοῦ
lit: and a thorn to his head
sm: and a thorn for his head

syn.mc: Continuation of the accusative list.
syn.svo: No finite verb. Object: ἄκανθαν. Dative phrase: τῇ κεφαλῇ αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: The bodily abuse is narrowed to the head.

src: καὶ τὰς καλὰς αὐτοῦ χεῖρας ἔδησας"
ana: καὶ τὰς καλὰς αὐτοῦ χεῖρας ἔδησας
lit: and you bound his beautiful hands
sm: and you bound his beautiful hands

syn.mc: Coordinate accusation clause.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἔδησας. Object: τὰς ... χεῖρας.
syn.rhet: The hands that should bless are bound instead.

src: αἵ σε ἔπλασαν ἀπὸ γῆς"
lit: which formed you from earth
sm: which formed you from earth

syn.mc: Relative clause modifying the hands in line 602.
syn.sub: The relative clause is subordinate to the noun phrase χεῖρας.
syn.svo: Subject: αἵ. Verb: ἔπλασαν. Object: σε. Source phrase: ἀπὸ γῆς.
syn.rhet: The creator's hands are the ones now bound.

src: καὶ τὸ καλὸν αὐτοῦ ἐκεῖνο στόμα τὸ ψωμίσαν σε ζωὴν
tok: aor act ptcp acc sg n
lit: and that beautiful mouth of his, the one that fed you life
sm: and that beautiful mouth of his, the one that fed you with life

syn.mc: Continuation of the accusative description.
syn.sub: The participial article phrase τὸ ψωμίσαν σε ζωὴν modifies στόμα.
syn.svo: Main noun phrase: τὸ καλὸν ... στόμα. Relative-style participial modifier: τὸ ψωμίσαν σε ζωὴν.
syn.ptc: ψωμίσαν is attributive to στόμα and recalls benefaction.
syn.rhet: The beneficent mouth is highlighted before the insult of gall.

src: ἐψώμισας χολήν."
lit: you fed gall.
sm: you gave him gall.

syn.mc: Short finite clause completing the contrast.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἐψώμισας. Object: χολήν.
syn.rhet: The benefaction of life is answered with bitterness.

src: καὶ ἀπέκτεινάς σου τὸν κύριον ἐν τῇ μεγάλῃ ἑορτῇ.
ana: καὶ ἀπέκτεινας σου τὸν κύριον ἐν τῇ μεγάλῃ ἑορτῇ.
lit: and you killed your Lord in the great feast.
sm: and you killed your Lord in the great feast.
emd: Normalized ἀπέκτεινάς to the expected aorist ἀπέκτεινας.

syn.mc: Main accusation clause closing the catalogue.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἀπέκτεινας. Object: τὸν κύριον. Locative phrase: ἐν τῇ μεγάλῃ ἑορτῇ.
syn.rhet: The charge is sharpened by the feast-day setting.

src: καὶ σὺ μὲν ἦσθα εὐφραινόμενος,
tok: pres mp ptcp nom sg m
lit: and you were indeed rejoicing,
sm: and you were rejoicing,

syn.mc: First half of an antithetical comparison.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verb: ἦσθα. Predicative participle: εὐφραινόμενος.
syn.rhet: The contrast with the suffering one begins.

src: ἐκεῖνος δὲ λιμώττων"
tok: pres act ptcp nom sg m
lit: but that one being hungry
sm: but he was hungry

syn.mc: Second half of the antithetical comparison.
syn.sub: No finite clause; the participle supplies the state.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐκεῖνος. Participle: λιμώττων.
syn.ptc: λιμώττων is predicative and opposed to εὐφραινόμενος.
syn.rhet: The contrast is now direct and symmetrical.

src: σὺ ἔπινες οἶνον καὶ ἄρτον ἤσθιες,
lit: you were drinking wine and were eating bread,
sm: you were drinking wine and eating bread,

syn.mc: Main clause of the comparison.
syn.svo: Subject: σὺ. Verbs: ἔπινες, ἤσθιες. Objects: οἶνον and ἄρτον.
syn.rhet: The addressee's abundance is set against the other's deprivation.

src: ἐκεῖνος δὲ ὄξος καὶ χολήν"
lit: but that one [had] vinegar and gall
sm: but he had vinegar and gall

syn.mc: Elliptical antithetical clause completing the comparison.
syn.sub: The verb is supplied from line 609.
syn.svo: Subject: ἐκεῖνος. Omitted verb understood from line 609. Objects: ὄξος and χολήν.
syn.rhet: The pairing closes the bitter contrast between luxury and suffering.
